title: Chapter 507
content:
Chapter 507: Chapter 507 Allocation

Liam finally reached the hotel, the convoy of black SUVs pulling up smoothly in front of the towering entrance. The building was a massive skyscraper that stretched high into the evening sky, its glass exterior reflecting the city lights like a giant mirror.

Vanessa leaned forward in her seat, eyes wide as she stared up through the window. "We get to stay in the penthouse?" she asked excitedly, already imagining the breathtaking view of the city skyline from that height. She could practically see herself standing by floor-to-ceiling windows, drink in hand, looking down at everything below.

Liam killed the engine and gave her a flat look. "Fuck no. I’m already there."

Vanessa’s excitement dropped instantly. "What?"

"You guys can take rooms on the floor below," he said casually, stepping out of the car.

Vanessa rolled her eyes dramatically as she climbed out after him, muttering something under her breath about stingy men.

Lilith and Liam exited the vehicle at the same time. Without wasting a single second, Lilith strode straight toward the hotel entrance like she owned the place. Liam followed right behind her, a small amused smile tugging at his lips. She didn’t even bother stopping at the receptionist desk to register. Instead, she turned her head slightly and looked at him with those golden eyes.

"We can both stay at the penthouse, right?" she asked.

The way she said it sounded like a question, but the look on her face made it anything but. Her expression was calm, almost innocent, but her eyes were saying something completely different — I am staying with you. Liam knew her too well. He didn’t dare say no.

He smiled softly. "Of course."

Lilith’s lips curved into a satisfied little smirk.

Liam turned toward the rest of the group, who were now gathering near the entrance. "Lana, Lilith and I will be in the penthouse. Help everyone settle into their rooms on the floor below."

Lana nodded without hesitation, and the others followed suit. Ann gave him a knowing look but didn’t say anything. Liam had no problem going to their rooms later since they would all be just one floor down.

He turned back and followed Lilith toward the private elevator. The doors opened smoothly, and they both stepped inside. The ride up was quiet, the soft hum of the elevator the only sound between them. When the doors opened again on the top floor, Lilith stepped out first, walking straight into the penthouse like she belonged there.

Liam followed, closing the door behind them.

Lilith slowly looked around the spacious living area, her sharp eyes scanning the luxurious furniture, the massive windows overlooking the city, and the elegant decor. After a long moment, she hummed thoughtfully.

"Hmmm... close enough."

Liam raised an eyebrow. "Close enough for what?"

Lilith turned to face him, a playful glint in her eyes. "Close enough to my penthouse back home."

Liam rolled his eyes. "Sure."

He shrugged off his long dark coat and tossed it over the back of a nearby couch. At the same time, Lilith kicked off her boots, letting them drop carelessly to the floor. She stretched slightly, the fitted black jacket and tight leather trousers hugging her body perfectly.

Liam stepped closer to her, his deep blue eyes tracing her figure. "Now don’t you feel hot in those tight leather pants?" he asked, voice low and teasing.

Lilith knew exactly what he was doing. This man was already trying to get her legs over his shoulders. She gave him a calm look, though the corner of her mouth twitched. "What do you mean? It’s freezing cold up here."

But Liam didn’t back off. He closed the distance between them, his hands gently settling on her waist. He could smell her familiar scent this close — warm, intoxicating, and dangerously addictive. He leaned in, whispering near her ear, "You sure about that?"

Lilith felt a strong urge to grab him and pull him into a heated kiss, but she controlled herself. Barely. Her golden-blonde hair shifted as she tilted her head slightly, maintaining that composed expression even as his hands started slowly circling her waist.
